mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production changes yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations during late-cycle phases, invalidating the supply-demand transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend pace signals strong aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: GDP growth above trend fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with rank correlation consistently below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, accelerating inflation forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High inflation readings coincide with positive 20-day equity returns in more than 65 percent of cases, or inflation premium fails to materialize in discount rate models.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 58 percent of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansion signal deteriorating construction demand and financial stress, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Falling housing starts fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with directional accuracy dropping below 48 percent during late-cycle macro regimes.
